scope.summary: Wires D5 idle-kill coverage for the pooled DB-crate path and the real pinned OracleDispatcher session path.
out_of_scope: Changing pool validate-on-checkout behavior; A4a owns moving recovery earlier than reconnect-then-replay.
Changing pinned-session recycle/reconnect behavior; A4e owns any future transparent recovery design.
Running a full workspace Cargo gate.
Changing the sibling rust-oracledb driver repository.

entry_points.name: D5 pinned dispatcher idle-kill lane
entry_points.trace: `ORACLEMCP_TEST_DSN=//localhost:1522/FREEPDB1 ORACLEMCP_TEST_USER=system ORACLEMCP_TEST_PASSWORD=<Docker-configured rust-oracledb-free password> CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo test -p oraclemcp --features live-xe --test live_dispatcher_idle_kill -- --nocapture` exited 0. The test killed the dispatcher's real pinned session and observed `oracle_connection_info` return connected=false with a typed TRANSIENT connection_error instead of silently rebinding.

entry_points.name: D5 pooled DB-crate idle-kill lane
entry_points.trace: `ORACLEMCP_TEST_DSN=//localhost:1522/FREEPDB1 ORACLEMCP_TEST_USER=system ORACLEMCP_TEST_PASSWORD=<Docker-configured rust-oracledb-free password> CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --features live-xe --test live_idle_kill -- --nocapture` exited 0. The test killed the single pooled session and observed replacement from sid 233 serial 9537 to sid 233 serial 40090.

entry_points.name: D5 formatting gate
entry_points.trace: `cargo fmt --all -- --check` exited 0 after the D5 test wiring.

entry_points.name: D5 staged shared-checkout guards
entry_points.trace: `scripts/swarm_discipline.sh struct-atomicity --staged`, `scripts/swarm_discipline.sh stale-delete-check --staged`, and `git diff --cached --check` exited 0 before the D5 commits.

limitations: The two Oracle kill lanes ran against the already-running local Free23 rig container and are recorded as scoped integration evidence, not as committed live-evidence artifacts.
The full workspace fmt/clippy/test/deny gate was not run; workspace-wide Cargo gates are serialized by the build lease.
The shared checkout contains dirty files outside this bead's scope, so only source-scoped tree cleanliness is claimed.
